input.command:
gh pr create --title "docs(adr): single-source the ADR index — CLAUDE.md §9 links docs/adr/README.md" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- CLAUDE.md §9 duplicated the `docs/adr/README.md` index and the two drifted — README still listed ADR 0026 as Proposed/Pending while the ADR file (and §9) say **Accepted, phase 1 built 2026-07-04 (#461)**. This PR makes the README index the single source of truth.
- §9 becomes a short pointer (the operating rules distilled from the ADRs already live in §4–§6/§10–§11); drops ~9KB of duplicated, drift-prone context from every AI session.
- `docs/adr/README.md` fixed: 0026 moved from Pending into the main index as Accepted; Pending keeps only 0015. The docs-consistency-guard agent's rule 4 now checks index-vs-ADR-file status agreement (and flags any re-grown §9 table).

## Linked issue

Refs — none (doc-consistency change, suggested by the maintainer)

## Type of change

- [x] docs — documentation only

## Checklist

- [x] **Manually tested locally** — links resolve; grep confirms the only operational §9-table dependents were docs-consistency-guard rules 4/65 (updated); ADR "Related" mentions of §9 remain valid (§9 still exists, as the pointer)
- [x] **Single functionality** — no unrelated changes piggybacked
- [x] **Conventional commit title**
- [x] **No secrets, credentials, or `.env` files committed**
- [ ] ~Black + Ruff + mypy~ — no Python changes
- [ ] ~Prettier + ESLint~ — no frontend changes
- [ ] ~Tests added/updated~ — markdown only
- [x] **Docs / ADRs updated** — this IS the docs change
- [x] **`docs/progress.md` — N/A** for this PR
- [x] **Security implications considered** — none

## Schema migration?

- [x] No schema change

## Manual test notes

Verified ADR 0026's own `Status:` line says Accepted (phase 1 built 2026-07-04) — the README Pending row was the stale copy. Grepped for `§9` across the repo: only `.claude/agents/docs-consistency-guard.md` depended on the table operationally (updated here); ADR Related-section mentions still point at a §9 that exists.

## Security / risk notes

None — markdown only.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

harness sleeps. This updates the §12 row to describe the new contract.

## Summary

- The harness gained the #609 dbt Core lineage project (ACA Job `dbt-lineage` on `dataq-cae`, cron `0 2 * * *`, artifacts → ADLS `raw/dbt/latest` for the #611 poller) and `harness_window.sh` (harness-side, not git-tracked — ADR 0021) was extended to match: `run --dbt` (resume→start→wait→re-suspend; needs live Snowflake, like `--adf`), and `status`/`stop` now cover the job so its cron is disarmed while the harness sleeps.
- This PR updates the CLAUDE.md §12 row describing that script's contract; nothing else.

Refs #609 / #611 (harness-side dbt leg; the DataQ-side provider is #611)

- [x] docs — documentation only

content.text: All merged and main is clean. Updating the dbt-harness memory to reflect that the leg is now built and script-managed:

result.output:
1	---
2	name: project_dbt_lineage_harness
3	description: "harness dbt Core lineage project (#609) — location, schemas, role, change-tracking caveat, artifact path for"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 4feb9be8-2ae1-4d50-be97-b9c4cbaead36
8	---
9	
10	Self-hosted **dbt Core** lineage project built 2026-07-05 (#609, v1.1 W2). Lives at
11	`~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/dbt/` (ADR 0021 — not git-tracked, not DataQ app code).
12	See [[project_harness_env]].
13	
14	**Shape:** `DATAQ_DB.RETAIL.{ORDERS_HEADER,ORDER_LINES,CUSTOMERS,PRODUCTS}` (base)
15	→ `ANALYTICS_STG.{stg_orders,stg_order_lines,stg_customers,stg_products}` (views)
16	→ `ANALYTICS.{MART_ORDER_REVENUE,MART_CUSTOMER_ORDERS}` (dynamic tables, target_lag 1h).
17	Marts dedupe with `qualify row_number()` because the ADF COPY loads append (base tables
18	carry duplicate grains — 800 orders / 200 customers doubled).
19	
20	**Role:** dbt runs as **DATAQ_LOADER, not ACCOUNTADMIN** (user decision). Needed one extra
21	grant — `CREATE SCHEMA ON DATABASE DATAQ_DB` (terraform `snowflake.tf` → `loader_create_schema`).
22	Loader owns the two ANALYTICS schemas so it grants SELECT/USAGE to DATAQ_READER via dbt
23	on-run-end hooks. profiles.yml reads `DBT_SNOWFLAKE_ROLE` (default DATAQ_LOADER) so secrets.sh's
24	`SNOWFLAKE_ROLE=ACCOUNTADMIN` can't re-escalate. secrets.sh exports SNOWFLAKE_DATABASE/WAREHOUSE
25	**set-but-empty** → profile uses `env_var(x,'') or 'DEFAULT'`, not env_var's default.
26	
27	**Change-tracking caveat (dynamic-table prereq):** loader can't enable change tracking (doesn't
28	own base tables). ORDERS_HEADER+CUSTOMERS → `change_tracking=true` in snowflake.tf; ORDER_LINES+
29	PRODUCTS → `dbt/bootstrap_change_tracking.sql` once as ACCOUNTADMIN. write_pandas overwrite drops
30	it, so re-run if `flow_a_snowflake_load` runs again.
31	
32	**Hosting:** one image, three homes — local `dbt build`; ACA Job `dbt-lineage` on `dataq-cae`
33	(terraform `dbt_job.tf`, cron `0 2 * * *`, **fires nightly** during the Azure window); docker-compose
34	post-wind-down. `run.sh` is a generic `dbt "$@"` wrapper (not `dbt build "$@"` — that double-verbed).
35	ACR Tasks are blocked on this sub → build locally `docker buildx --platform linux/amd64 --push`.
36	
37	**Artifacts (for #611 provider poll + #596 lineage):** `upload_artifacts.py` → ADLS
38	`adls://dataqharness3erlgd/raw/dbt/latest/{manifest,run_results}.json` (+ `runs/<UTC-ts>/`).
39	
40	**Verified live 2026-07-05:** local + ACA `dbt build` both PASS=17; DataQ suite (3 expectations +
41	freshness on ORDER_TS, ~15h) green against MART_ORDER_REVENUE via the "Snowflake — Retail
42	(DATAQ_READER)" connection `6729c4f9…` + W1 admin PAT. Next: **#611** (dbt as third
43	OrchestrationProvider) is unblocked — it polls this run_results.json. See [[project_live_connections_and_demo]].
44

content.text:
All done — everything merged. Here's the full picture:

## Merged (3 PRs, all squash-merged with green CI)

- **#646 — agent optimizations** (`qa-verifier` + `ui-tester`): the original work plus a `fixup:` commit addressing all nine doc findings from the /code-review pass (scenario renumbering, source-of-truth pointers for the provider list, the webhook-secret local prerequisite, the too-narrow `--label bug` dedup filter, the un-swept new routes, screenshot-lifecycle contradiction, `:connectionId` param name, and the false-precision thresholds).
- **#648 — ADR index single-sourced** (your suggestion): CLAUDE.md §9 is now a short pointer to [docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md) instead of a duplicate 29-row table (~9KB dropped from every session's context). The drift you suspected was real and live: the README still listed **ADR 0026 as Proposed/Pending** while the ADR file says Accepted — fixed as part of making the index the single source. The docs-consistency-guard agent's rule 4 was retargeted to check index-vs-ADR-file status agreement.
- **#649 — §12 harness row refreshed** for the dbt leg (below).

Also filed **[#647](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/647)** (bug, v1.1 W2) — a real backend bug the review's cross-file tracer found and verified: `admin_service.webhook_configs()` still has the pre-ADR-0029 two-provider `if adf / else airflow` branch, so **dbt connections are reported as airflow** with the wrong inbound URL and signing-secret name on the admin page. The issue carries file:line evidence, git-history root cause, and acceptance criteria.

## Harness script — your hunch was right

`harness_window.sh` had zero dbt awareness while the `dbt-lineage` ACA Job (built today, #609) is deployed with a live nightly cron. I updated the script (harness-side, `bash -n` clean): `run --dbt` fires the job manually (resume → start → wait → re-suspend; needs live Snowflake, like `--adf`, and runs last so marts refresh from a same-window load), and `status`/`stop` now cover the job so `stop` disarms its cron like the mockdata ones. A review agent verified the CLAUDE.md row against the actual script and terraform before #649 merged.

**One decision for you:** the `dbt-lineage` cron (`0 2 * * *` UTC) has been armed since the job was created today — I didn't suspend it unilaterally in case you want tonight's run for #611 artifact testing. Say the word and I'll suspend it now; otherwise the next `harness_window.sh stop` takes care of it.
